search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae cough hemoptysis telehealth guideline PDF
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Emergency treatment content was not mapped.
Anticoagulation management and reversal require separate exact sources and clinician governance.
population_applicability: Partial: DHA cough applicability does not state an age boundary and NICE recommendations contain age and smoking qualifiers.
setting_applicability: Partial: direct for DHA telehealth cough red-flag and referral documentation plus NICE suspected-cancer recognition, not emergency haemoptysis management.
UAE_applicability: Direct for DHA-licensed telehealth fields; NICE suspected-cancer evidence requires UAE clinician review and local cancer pathways.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 cough guideline and NICE NG12 updated 2026-04-15 were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer DHA cough issue was identified; NG12 remains current.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped cough, sputum, breathlessness, chest pain, systemic symptoms, smoking, and clinician-assessed safety fields as prompts only.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists cough features that require serious-condition assessment or face-to-face escalation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped cough or sputum, chest-pain or breathlessness, systemic-symptom, smoking, and prior-episode context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies cough duration, quality, timing, risk factors, and associated-symptom history domains.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed chest radiography, blood, and sputum fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-selected blood tests, chest radiography, peak-flow diary, pulmonary function testing, sputum testing, ECG, and specialist-led advanced imaging.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-decided referral and safety documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led emergency, family-medicine, and pulmonary referral according to the documented cough context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped haemoptysis identity and aliases with recommendation-specific age and smoking qualifiers preserved.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician recognition of unexplained haemoptysis and age- and smoking-context combinations involving unexplained cough, fatigue, breathlessness, chest pain, weight loss, appetite loss, recurrent chest infection, clubbing, lymphadenopathy, or chest signs.

unresolved_source_gaps: No exact source was accepted for haemoptysis amount or colour, massive-haemoptysis thresholds, chest trauma, anticoagulation risk or reversal, TB-negative summary wording, or fixed follow-up.
Known malignancy risk, broad vitals and examination fields, CT chest, coagulation testing, generic clinician plan, PRN wording, and the combined structured history remain unsupported.
Bleeding severity, airway risk, diagnosis, investigation selection, emergency disposition, treatment, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
